title: Turkey's Erdogan says NATO summit must emphasise unity and resilience
gist: Ahead of hosting the July 7-8 NATO summit in Ankara, Erdogan pressed for lifting restrictions on defense-industry trade and for Turkey's inclusion in European security initiatives, amid alliance tensions over burden-sharing and US complaints that allies stayed out of reopening the Strait of Hormuz.

insights.claim: Erdogan is using Turkey's role as NATO summit host (Ankara, July 7-8, 32 leaders) to push for lifting US and European restrictions on defense-industry trade imposed over Ankara's ties with Russia and regional policy differences.
insights.speaker: Al-Monitor
insights.so_what: If embargoes ease, Turkish defense firms gain access to Western supply chains and joint programs — a catalyst to watch for Turkey's defense sector.
insights.quote: we must lift restrictions on defence industry trade while carrying out burden sharing in a balanced and fair manner
insights.locator: If we want to combat the tests we are facing

insights.claim: Turkey is pressing to join all European defense and security initiatives, positioning itself as a contributor to the alliance's 'European pillar' whose benefits it says are being overlooked.
insights.speaker: Al-Monitor
insights.so_what: Turkish entry into European rearmament programs would shift procurement flows and industrial partnerships across the EU defense buildout.
insights.quote: we have the will to join all defence and security initiatives in the continent
insights.locator: As a nation that has a say in the development

insights.claim: The summit convenes amid alliance tensions over burden-sharing, defense spending, and US complaints that allies failed to help reopen the Strait of Hormuz during the US-Israeli war with Iran.
insights.speaker: Al-Monitor
insights.so_what: US grievance over allies' Hormuz absence signals widening burden-sharing rifts — a backdrop for further pressure on European defense budgets.
insights.quote: U.S. complaints about allies' lack of involvement in re-opening the Strait of Hormuz during the U.S. and Israeli war with Iran

insights.claim: Erdogan said allies will discuss the wars in Iran, Ukraine, and Gaza at the summit, and expects more allied support to end the PKK's 40-year insurgency.
insights.speaker: Al-Monitor
insights.so_what: Three active wars on the summit agenda underscore an unusually high geopolitical risk backdrop for the July meeting.
insights.quote: the allies would also discuss the wars in Iran, Ukraine, and Gaza during their meeting
insights.locator: He said the allies would also discuss
